title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, a former Boston teacher, sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation, including receiving child pornography.
Gavin used Discord to engage in sexualized conversations with minors and solicited explicit images from at least 20 girls.
Authorities found 147 files of child sexual abuse material on Gavin's cellphone, depicting minors aged 5 to 17.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots are considering Tyreek Hill, who is recovering from a leg injury, to bolster their receiving corps.
Marquise Brown is another potential fit for the Patriots' receiver lineup, known for his speed and big play ability.
Linebacker Bobby Okereke, known for his versatility, would strengthen the Patriots' defense amidst uncertainty in their current linebacker group.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 24 points, 10 rebounds, and two blocks in a 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum contributed 20 points despite ongoing struggles with his three-point shooting, going 2 for 9 from beyond the arc.
Boston's defense contained Wizards stars Alex Sarr and Trae Young, limiting their scoring and impact on the game.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick's Day weekend with a concert on March 13 at MGM Music Hall.
Openers included The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, enhancing the event's vibrant atmosphere.
Band members Ken Casey, Kevin Rheault, and Jeff DaRosa engaged the crowd with dynamic performances.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made its NWSL debut with over 30,000 fans in attendance at Gillette Stadium.
Gotham FC defeated Legacy FC 1-0, with eight yellow cards issued, tying an NWSL record for a single match.
Legacy FC head coach Filipa Patão emphasized the need for physical play, while acknowledging room for tactical improvement.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick’s Day weekend with a concert at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, 2026.
The band performed both old and new songs, including the live debut of "Only the Strong" from their EP "New England Forever."
Dropkick Murphys' set included political statements, such as a protest against ICE during the performance of "Citizen I.C.E."

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at 11, receiving vital support from Joslin Diabetes Center.
He aims to support others with Type 1 Diabetes and raise awareness by completing the marathon on April 21.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter hits a milestone with her fifth Oscar nomination for 'Sinners,' becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter made history as the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019 and hopes to secure a third win.
Carter uses knowledge from her Massachusetts roots and local antique resources for projects, demonstrating a strong connection to New England.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools could face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override is not approved by voters.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain staffing and programs, risking cuts to programs and increased class sizes.
Brookline voters will decide on the property-tax override during the annual town election on May 5.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider the PETS Act to improve animal welfare and regulate pet sales, targeting puppy mills.
The PETS Act would ban pet shop sales of dogs and cats, encouraging partnerships with shelters for adoptions.
The bill addresses housing and insurance barriers, expanding pet ownership access in state-aided public housing.
